title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Ports: tp.hs.dead.setup.sas, tp.hs.dead_uses_setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas, tp.hs.dead.conditional.setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.conditional.uses_setup.sas, tp.hs.dead.compare_benefit.setup.sas, tp.hs.uslife_estimates_generate_stratify_.age.sas, tp.hs.uslife_generates_matched_estimates.sas R equivalents: hazard_plot(), survival_difference_plot() tp.hs.* family extends tp.hp.dead.* using %hazpred macro generate patient-specific parametric survival predictions fitted multivariable hazard model. setup templates compute store: Cumulative hazard per patient last follow-(observed vs. expected goodness--fit tests via %chisqgf). Individual survivorship curves fine time grid (mean-curve aggregation PROC SUMMARY). Subsequent use templates stratify aggregated mean curves covariate overlay Kaplan-Meier estimates %kaplan. resulting SAS datasets map directly hazard_plot() R: Reproduces core figure tp.hs.dead_uses_setup.sas tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas: mean parametric survival curve (solid line confidence band) overlaid Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates (symbols error bars). tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas overlays curves depth--invasion groups (tdepth = 1, 2, 3). Pass group_col hazard_plot() supply matched sample_hazard_data() / sample_hazard_empirical() calls groups vector whose names become legend labels. tp.hs.dead.conditional.setup.sas computes individual survivorship curves starting time hospital discharge rather operation date. conditional survival S(t | discharge) S(t) / S(t_discharge) patient. R, hazard_plot() call used; x-axis label data preparation differ. tp.hs.uslife_* templates call %usmatchd generate age-, sex-, race-matched US population life-table survival curves patient. sample_life_table() provides representative matched curves stratified age group use R examples. tp.hs.dead.compare_benefit.setup.sas computes, patient, difference predicted survival fixed time point (5 years) two treatment arms (e.g. ASA vs. ASA). distribution individual differences shows benefits much. Use survival_difference_plot() plot mean curve time.
code: dat_hp <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_hp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_hp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster") dat_strat <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 10,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) ) emp_strat <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) )  hazard_plot(   dat_strat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_strat,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster") dat_cond <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_cond <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_cond,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_cond,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Discharge", y = "Survival after Discharge (%)") +   hv_theme("poster") dat_age <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 10,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) emp_age <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) lt <- sample_life_table(   age_groups = c("<60", "60\u201385", "\u226585"),   age_mids   = c(50, 72, 88),   time_max   = 10 )  hazard_plot(   dat_age,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp_age,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Age group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hv_theme("poster") diff_dat <- sample_survival_difference_data(   n      = 500,   groups = c("No ASA" = 1.0, "ASA" = 0.75) )  survival_difference_plot(   diff_dat,   lower_col = "diff_lower",   upper_col = "diff_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",                       colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-5, 40),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: Reproduces core figure tp.hs.dead_uses_setup.sas tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas: mean parametric survival curve (solid line confidence band) overlaid Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates (symbols error bars).
code: dat_hp <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_hp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_hp,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_hp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: tp.hs.dead.procedure.tdepth.sas overlays curves depth--invasion groups (tdepth = 1, 2, 3). Pass group_col hazard_plot() supply matched sample_hazard_data() / sample_hazard_empirical() calls groups vector whose names become legend labels.
code: dat_strat <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 10,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) ) emp_strat <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("pT1" = 0.4, "pT2" = 1.0, "pT3" = 1.8) )  hazard_plot(   dat_strat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_strat,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("pT1" = "steelblue", "pT2" = "forestgreen", "pT3" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

text: tp.hs.dead.conditional.setup.sas computes individual survivorship curves starting time hospital discharge rather operation date. conditional survival S(t | discharge) S(t) / S(t_discharge) patient. R, hazard_plot() call used; x-axis label data preparation differ.
code: dat_cond <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp_cond <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  hazard_plot(   dat_cond,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp_cond,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 10),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Discharge", y = "Survival after Discharge (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: SAS Template Migration Guide: Finding Your Plot in R
text: tp.hs.uslife_* templates call %usmatchd generate age-, sex-, race-matched US population life-table survival curves patient. sample_life_table() provides representative matched curves stratified age group use R examples.
code: dat_age <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 10,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) emp_age <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<60" = 0.4, "60\u201385" = 1.0, "\u226585" = 2.0) ) lt <- sample_life_table(   age_groups = c("<60", "60\u201385", "\u226585"),   age_mids   = c(50, 72, 88),   time_max   = 10 )  hazard_plot(   dat_age,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp_age,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Age group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<60" = "steelblue", "60\u201385" = "forestgreen",                "\u226585" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: Install GitHub using remotes: Apply HVI theme ggplot2 figure one line: Export result directly editable PowerPoint slide:
code: remotes::install_github("ehrlinger/hvtiPlotR") library(ggplot2) library(hvtiPlotR)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point() +   hv_theme("manuscript") p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() + hv_theme("ppt")  save_ppt(   object     = p,   template   = system.file("ClevelandClinic.pptx", package = "hvtiPlotR"),   powerpoint = "outputs/figure1.pptx" )

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: Use hv_theme() apply supported style, call named aliases directly:
code: p + hv_theme("manuscript")   # via generic p + theme_man()                # direct alias p + hv_theme("ppt")          # dark PPT p + hv_theme("light_ppt")    # light PPT p + hv_theme("poster")       # poster

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: hvtiPlotR 2.0 uses two-step API. constructor (hv_*()) shapes data returns S3 object; plot() renders bare ggplot. Add scale_*, labs(), annotate(), theme usual + operator.
code: km <- hv_survival(sample_survival_data()) plot(km) + hv_theme("manuscript") km$tables$risk                         # risk-table data frame

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
code: mh <- hv_mirror_hist(sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 2000),                   group_labels = c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR")) plot(mh) + hv_theme("manuscript")

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R

title: Porting HVTI plot templates and methodology documentation to ggplot2 package in R
text: Pull requests welcome! Please read CONTRIBUTING.md full guide, covers: Track — porting SAS template (biostatisticians analysts adding new plot function sample-data generator) Track B — package infrastructure (R developers working dependencies, CI, testing, CRAN compliance) Quick-start: Open issue discuss new port writing code, tag @ehrlinger review pull requests.
code: devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE) devtools::load_all() devtools::check()

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: Superseded. hazard_plot() superseded S3 constructor hv_hazard() plus plot.hv_hazard(). Plots pre-computed parametric survival, hazard, cumulative-hazard curve Weibull (parametric) model, optionally overlaid Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates population life-table reference. Covers complete family tp.hp.dead.* SAS templates. SAS column mapping: x_col          ← YEARS / iv_dead estimate_col   ← SSURVIV (survival), hazard (%/yr), cumhaz lower_col      ← SCLLSURV / cll_p95 upper_col      ← SCLUSURV / clu_p95 group_col      ← treatment/group indicator variable empirical      ← plout / acpdms KM output dataset reference      ← smatched life-table dataset Returns bare ggplot object; compose scale_colour_*, scale_y_continuous(), labs(), hv_theme().

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
code: hazard_plot(   curve_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL,   empirical = NULL,   emp_x_col = x_col,   emp_estimate_col = "estimate",   emp_lower_col = NULL,   emp_upper_col = NULL,   emp_group_col = group_col,   emp_geom = c("point", "step"),   reference = NULL,   ref_x_col = x_col,   ref_estimate_col = estimate_col,   ref_group_col = NULL,   ci_alpha = 0.2,   line_width = 1,   point_size = 2,   point_shape = 1L,   errorbar_width = 0.25 )

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: curve_data Data frame parametric predictions (fine grid). Typical source: SAS predict dataset exported CSV, sample_hazard_data(). x_col Name time (age) column. Corresponds YEARS / iv_dead SAS. Default "time". estimate_col Name predicted-value column. Use "survival" survival plot, "hazard" hazard-rate plot, "cumhaz" cumulative-hazard plot. Corresponds SSURVIV, hazard, cumhaz SAS output. Default "survival". lower_col Name lower CI column curve_data, NULL ribbon. Corresponds SCLLSURV / cll_p95. Default NULL. upper_col Name upper CI column curve_data, NULL. Corresponds SCLUSURV / clu_p95. Default NULL. group_col Name stratification column curve_data, NULL single curve. Default NULL. empirical Optional data frame Kaplan-Meier empirical estimates (discrete time points). Corresponds SAS plout / acpdms dataset. See sample_hazard_empirical(). Default NULL. emp_x_col Column name x empirical. Default: x_col. emp_estimate_col Column name y empirical. Default: estimate_col. emp_lower_col Column name error-bar lower bound empirical, NULL error bars. Default NULL. emp_upper_col Column name error-bar upper bound empirical, NULL. Default NULL. emp_group_col Column name grouping empirical. Default: group_col. emp_geom Geom used empirical overlay: "point" (default, open circles) "step" (Kaplan-Meier step function). reference Optional data frame population life-table survival curves. Corresponds SAS smatched / hmatched datasets. See sample_life_table(). Rendered dashed lines. Default NULL. ref_x_col Column name x reference. Default: x_col. ref_estimate_col Column name y reference. Default: estimate_col. ref_group_col Column name used vary linetype reference curves (e.g. age group). Default NULL. ci_alpha Transparency parametric CI ribbon ([0, 1]). Default 0.20. line_width Width parametric curve line. SAS width=3 corresponds roughly 1.2. Default 1.0. point_size Size empirical overlay points. Default 2.0. point_shape Shape code empirical points. 1 = open circle (SAS symbol=circle); 0 = open square (SAS symbol=square). Default 1. errorbar_width Width error bars empirical points. Default 0.25.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
text: SAS templates: tp.hp.dead.sas, tp.hp.dead.tkdn.stratified.sas, tp.hp.dead.age_with_population_life_table.sas, tp.hp.dead.uslife.stratifed.sas, tp.hp.dead.matching_weight.sas, tp.hp.dead.limited_FET.mtwt.sas, tp.hp.event.weighted.sas, tp.hp.repeated.event.weighted.sas, tp.hp.repeated_events.sas, tp.hp.mcs.mod.dead.devseq_nlvlv12.scallop.sas.

title: Parametric Hazard / Survival Plot — hazard_plot
code: library(ggplot2)  dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  # --- (1) Basic survival curve + KM overlay ------------------------------- # Matches tp.hp.dead.sas (survival panel). # SAS: SSURVIV on y-axis, SCLLSURV/SCLUSURV for CI, plout circles + bars. hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (2) Hazard rate curve + KM overlay ---------------------------------- # Matches tp.hp.dead.sas (hazard panel). # SAS: hazard on y-axis (%/year). KM empirical hazard not shown here # (the empirical overlay in SAS is the survival-based dot plot; for hazard, # only the parametric curve is typically shown). hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col = "hazard",   lower_col    = "haz_lower",   upper_col    = "haz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%/yr")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Hazard (%/year)") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 128 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).   # --- (3) Cumulative hazard (tp.hp.event.weighted.sas) -------------------- # For readmission / repeated-event analyses: cumhaz = -log(S)*100. # SAS: Nelson-Aalen cumulative hazard. R: use cumhaz column from predict. hazard_plot(   dat,   estimate_col  = "cumhaz",   lower_col     = "cumhaz_lower",   upper_col     = "cumhaz_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("darkorange"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Cumulative Hazard (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (4) Stratified survival (tp.hp.dead.tkdn.stratified.sas) ------------ # Two groups (e.g. Takedown vs No Takedown) with different colours and # linetypes. KM empirical overlay uses matching colours. dat2 <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 400, time_max = 10,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) emp2 <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 400, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) hazard_plot(   dat2,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp2,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Esophagostomy", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (5) Life table overlay (tp.hp.dead.age_with_population_life_table) --- # Age-stratified study curves + US population life table reference (dashed). # SAS: smatched as dashed overlay; different symbols per age group. dat3 <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 600, time_max = 12,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65-80" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) emp3 <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 600, time_max = 12, n_bins = 6,   groups = c("<65" = 0.5, "65-80" = 1.0, "\u226580" = 1.8) ) lt <- sample_life_table(time_max = 12)  hazard_plot(   dat3,   estimate_col     = "survival",   lower_col        = "surv_lower",   upper_col        = "surv_upper",   group_col        = "group",   empirical        = emp3,   emp_lower_col    = "lower",   emp_upper_col    = "upper",   reference        = lt,   ref_estimate_col = "survival",   ref_group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65-80" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     name = "Age Group"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("<65" = "steelblue", "65-80" = "forestgreen",                "\u226580" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = seq(0, 12, 2)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)",        caption = "Dashed lines: US population life table") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (6) Multivariable risk profiles (tp.hp.dead.ideal_multivariable) ----- # Patient profiles with distinct covariate combinations (good vs poor risk). dat_risk <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 500, time_max = 8,   groups = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)" = 0.4,              "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = 1.8) ) hazard_plot(   dat_risk,   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col    = "surv_lower",   upper_col    = "surv_upper",   group_col    = "group" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)"              = "steelblue",                "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Patient profile"   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Ideal (young, stage IIIA)"              = "steelblue",                "Poor (elderly, stage IIIB, palliation)" = "firebrick"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 8), breaks = 0:8) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Brain Metastases",        y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (7) Propensity-weighted / matched groups ----------------------------- # tp.hp.dead.matching_weight.sas / tp.hp.dead.limited_FET.mtwt.sas. # Propensity weighting is applied upstream (before plotting); the plot call # is identical to the basic stratified survival plot above. dat_match <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 300, time_max = 7,   groups = c("Limited FET" = 1.0, "Extended FET" = 0.72) ) emp_match <- sample_hazard_empirical(   n = 300, time_max = 7, n_bins = 5,   groups = c("Limited FET" = 1.0, "Extended FET" = 0.72) ) hazard_plot(   dat_match,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp_match,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Limited FET" = "steelblue", "Extended FET" = "#8B4513"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("Limited FET" = "steelblue", "Extended FET" = "#8B4513"),     guide  = "none"   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 7), breaks = 0:7) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years after Repair", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (8) Device sequencing (tp.hp.mcs.mod.dead.devseq) ------------------- # Survival conditioned on surviving the non-LVAD phase, then the LVAD phase. # In R: generate two separate prediction segments and rbind them. dat_dev1 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 200, time_max = 0.038,                                 shape = 0.5, scale = 0.5) dat_dev1$device <- "Non-LVAD (first 2 weeks)" dat_dev2 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 200, time_max = 0.25,                                 shape = 1.0, scale = 1.5) dat_dev2$time   <- dat_dev2$time + 0.038 dat_dev2$device <- "LVAD" dat_dev  <- rbind(dat_dev1, dat_dev2) hazard_plot(   dat_dev,   estimate_col = "survival",   group_col    = "device" ) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("Non-LVAD (first 2 weeks)" = "steelblue", "LVAD" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.29),                       breaks = c(0, 0.038, 0.08, 0.13, 0.19, 0.25),                       labels = c("0", "2w", "1m", "6w", "2m", "3m")) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100),                       labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Time on Device", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (9) Save (dontrun) -------------------------------------------------- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- hazard_plot(dat, estimate_col = "survival",                  lower_col = "surv_lower", upper_col = "surv_upper",                  empirical = emp,                  emp_lower_col = "lower", emp_upper_col = "upper") +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster") ggplot2::ggsave("survival.pdf", p, width = 11.5, height = 8) } # }  # --- Global theme + RColorBrewer (set once per session) ------------------ if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ old <- ggplot2::theme_set(hv_theme_manuscript()) hazard_plot(   dat2,   estimate_col  = "survival",   lower_col     = "surv_lower",   upper_col     = "surv_upper",   group_col     = "group",   empirical     = emp2,   emp_lower_col = "lower",   emp_upper_col = "upper" ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette   = "Set1", guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                               labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") ggplot2::theme_set(old) } # }  # See vignette("plot-decorators", package = "hvtiPlotR") for theming, # colour scales, annotation labels, and saving plots.

what: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
title: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
text: Validates wide alluvial-format data frame returns hv_alluvial object.  Call plot.hv_alluvial result obtain bare ggplot2 alluvial diagram can decorate colour scales, axis labels, hv_theme.

title: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
code: hv_alluvial(data, axes, y_col = "freq", fill_col = NULL, axis_labels = NULL)

title: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
text: data data frame wide alluvial format: one row per axis-value combination, numeric weight column, one column per axis. axes Character vector column names use axes, left--right display order. Minimum two columns. y_col Name numeric weight column (counts proportions). Default "freq". fill_col Name column map flow fill colour aesthetics, NULL single fill. Default NULL. axis_labels Character vector axis labels x-axis, length axes. Defaults axes (column names).

title: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
text: object class c("hv_alluvial", "hv_data"): $data validated input data frame. $meta Named list: axes, y_col, fill_col, axis_labels, n_axes, n_obs. $tables Empty list.

title: Prepare alluvial / Sankey diagram data for plotting — hv_alluvial
code: dta  <- sample_alluvial_data(n = 300, seed = 42) axes <- c("pre_ar", "procedure", "post_ar")  # 1. Build data object al <- hv_alluvial(dta, axes = axes, y_col = "freq", fill_col = "pre_ar") al  # prints axes and observation count #> <hv_alluvial> #>   Axes (3)    : pre_ar → procedure → post_ar #>   Weight col  : freq #>   Fill col    : pre_ar #>   N rows      : 25  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_alluvial p <- plot(al)  # 3. Decorate: fill/colour palettes, axis labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     name = "Pre-op AR"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "goldenrod",                Moderate = "darkorange",                Severe   = "firebrick"),     guide = "none"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(y = "Patients (n)",                 title = "AV Regurgitation: Pre- to Post-operative") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes. #> Warning: Some strata appear at multiple axes.

what: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
title: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
text: Validates orders long-format standardized-mean-difference data frame covariate balance plot, returns hv_balance object. Call plot.hv_balance result obtain bare ggplot2 object can decorate colour, shape, axis scales, hv_theme.

title: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
code: hv_balance(   data,   variable_col = "variable",   group_col = "group",   std_diff_col = "std_diff",   var_levels = NULL,   threshold = 10 )

title: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
text: data data frame long format one row per covariate \(\times\) group combination.  Wide-format data must reshaped first (e.g. tidyr::pivot_longer()). variable_col Name column containing covariate labels. Default "variable". group_col Name column identifying comparison group (e.g. "match" / "match"). Default "group". std_diff_col Name numeric column holding standardized mean difference values.  Default "std_diff". var_levels Character vector controlling display order covariates y-axis.  first element appears bottom. Defaults order first appearance data[[variable_col]]. threshold Numeric; absolute SMD value dotted reference lines drawn (\(\pm\)threshold).  Default 10.

title: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
text: object class c("hv_balance", "hv_data") — list three elements: $data input data frame cb_index column added y-axis positioning. $meta Named list: variable_col, group_col, std_diff_col, var_levels, threshold, n_vars, n_groups. $tables Empty list (accessory tables).

title: Prepare covariate balance data for plotting — hv_balance
code: library(ggplot2) dta <- sample_covariate_balance_data()  # 1. Build data object cb <- hv_balance(dta) cb  # prints variable count, group count, threshold #> <hv_balance> #>   Variables   : 12 #>   Groups      : 2 (Before match, After match) #>   SMD col     : std_diff #>   Threshold   : ±10  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_balance p <- plot(cb)  # 3. Decorate: colour/shape palettes, x-axis range, labels, theme p +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Before match" = "red4", "After match" = "blue3"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Before match" = 17L, "After match" = 15L),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-45, 35), breaks = seq(-40, 30, 10)) +   labs(x = "Standardized difference (%)", y = "") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

what: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
text: Classifies y_col using eda_classify_var, pre-processes categorical levels (adding explicit "(Missing)" level), returns hv_eda object.  Call plot.hv_eda result obtain bare ggplot2 barplot scatter plot can decorate colour scales hv_theme.

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
code: hv_eda(   data,   x_col = "year",   y_col = "ef",   y_label = NULL,   unique_limit = 6L,   show_percent = FALSE )

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
text: data Data frame; one row per observation. x_col Name reference (time/grouping) column.  Used x-axis scatter bar plots.  Default "year". y_col Name variable plot.  Default "ef". y_label Optional human-readable label variable, used plot title, y-axis label (continuous), fill-legend name (categorical).  NULL (default), y_col used. unique_limit Integer threshold passed eda_classify_var distinguish categorical continuous numeric columns.  Default 6. show_percent Logical; categorical plots, use proportions (position = "fill") instead counts (position = "stack")? Default FALSE.

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
text: object class c("hv_eda", "hv_data"): $data Pre-processed data frame ready plotting.  continuous variables: two columns x y.  categorical variables: columns x (factor) fill (factor "(Missing)" explicit level). $meta Named list: x_col, y_col, y_label, var_type ("Cont", "Cat_Num", "Cat_Char"), show_percent, n_obs. $tables continuous variables: rug_data — rows y_col NA, used rug layer.  Empty list categorical variables.

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
text: Iterate variables lapply() selecting columns eda_select_vars.

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
text: R templates: tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots.R, tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R; helper: Barplot_Scatterplot_Function.R (Function_DataPlotting(), Order_Variables()).

title: Prepare EDA data for a single variable — hv_eda
code: dta <- sample_eda_data(n = 300, seed = 42)  # 1. Build data object (binary categorical) ed <- hv_eda(dta, x_col = "year", y_col = "male", y_label = "Sex") ed  # prints var_type and observation count #> <hv_eda> #>   Variable    : male  [Cat_Num] #>   Label       : Sex #>   x col       : year #>   N obs       : 300  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_eda p <- plot(ed)  # 3. Decorate: fill palette, x-axis breaks, labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(     values = c("0" = "steelblue", "1" = "firebrick", "(Missing)" = "grey80"),     labels = c("0" = "Female", "1" = "Male", "(Missing)" = "Missing"),     name   = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(breaks = seq(2005, 2020, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Surgery Year", y = "Count") +   hv_theme("poster")   # Continuous variable -- same 3-step pattern ed2 <- hv_eda(dta, x_col = "op_years", y_col = "ef",                 y_label = "Ejection Fraction (%)") plot(ed2) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("firebrick"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +   hv_theme("poster")   # Variable selection + lapply (varnames template pattern) cont_vars <- c(ef = "Ejection Fraction (%)",                lv_mass = "LV Mass Index (g/m\u00b2)",                peak_grad = "Peak Gradient (mmHg)") sub_cont <- eda_select_vars(dta, c("op_years", names(cont_vars))) p_cont <- lapply(names(cont_vars), function(cn) {   plot(hv_eda(sub_cont, x_col = "op_years", y_col = cn,                y_label = cont_vars[[cn]])) +     ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +     ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 15, 5)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Years from First Surgery Year") +     hv_theme("poster") }) p_cont[[1]]

what: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
title: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
text: Validates dates, builds per-patient follow-frames, returns hv_followup object containing data death panel (type = "followup") , optionally, event panel (type = "event").  Call plot.hv_followup result obtain bare ggplot2 object.

title: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
code: hv_followup(   data,   iv_opyrs_col = "iv_opyrs",   death_col = "dead",   death_time_col = "iv_dead",   event_col = NULL,   event_time_col = NULL,   death_for_event_col = NULL,   origin_year = 1990,   study_start = as.Date("1990-01-01"),   study_end = as.Date("2019-12-31"),   close_date = as.Date("2021-08-06"),   tolower_names = TRUE,   death_levels = c("Alive", "Dead"),   event_levels = c("No event", "Non-fatal event", "Death"),   segment_drop = 0.2 )

title: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
text: data data frame one row per patient. See sample_goodness_followup_data. iv_opyrs_col Name operation-year column. Default "iv_opyrs". death_col Name binary death-indicator column. Default "dead". death_time_col Name time--death column. Default "iv_dead". event_col Name non-fatal event indicator column. Required compute event panel (type = "event"). Default NULL (event panel unavailable). event_time_col Name time--event column. Required event_col supplied.  Default NULL. death_for_event_col Name death column use specifically event panel (defaults death_col NULL). origin_year Integer; calendar year used y-axis origin. Default 1990. study_start Start study period.  Default .Date("1990-01-01"). study_end End study enrolment.  Default .Date("2019-12-31"). close_date Data close date.  Must \(\geq\) study_end.  Default .Date("2021-08-06"). tolower_names Logical; whether lower-case column names materialising data.  Default TRUE. death_levels Length-2 character vector labelling two death states (alive first).  Default c("Alive", "Dead"). event_levels Length-3 character vector event panel (event-free, non-fatal event, death). Default c("event", "Non-fatal event", "Death"). segment_drop Numeric; vertical offset (years) segment endpoint follow-point.  Default 0.2.

title: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
text: object class c("hv_followup", "hv_data"): $data Per-patient data frame death panel. $meta Column names, date parameters, state levels, has_event flag. $tables Named list diagonal (study-period reference diagonal) , event columns supplied, event_data.

title: Prepare goodness-of-follow-up data for plotting — hv_followup
code: dta <- sample_goodness_followup_data()  # 1. Build data object gf <- hv_followup(dta) gf  # prints follow-up summary #> <hv_followup> #>   N patients  : 300 #>   Study period: 1990-01-01 – 2019-12-31 (close: 2021-08-06) #>   Death col   : dead / iv_dead #>   Panels      : followup  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_followup p <- plot(gf)  # 3. Decorate: colour palette, axis labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c("Alive" = "steelblue", "Dead" = "firebrick"),     name = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # With event panel -- same 3-step pattern gf2 <- hv_followup(dta, event_col = "ev_event", event_time_col = "iv_event") plot(gf2, type = "event") +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c("No event" = "blue", "Non-fatal event" = "green3",                "Death" = "red"),     name = NULL   ) +   ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(1, 2, 4), name = NULL) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Operation Date", y = "Follow-up (years)") +   hv_theme("poster")

what: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
text: Given fitted ggplot, returns width height values , passed ggplot2::ggsave(), resulting file panel content area matching width / height. "panel content area" rectangular bounding box gtable cells tagged panel — .e., smallest rectangle encloses every plotting panel. Whatever grobs fall inside rectangle (e.g., inter-panel gutters strip rows sit facet rows) counted part target; everything outside (axes, axis titles, plot title, caption, legend, plot margins, strips sit /beside panel block facet_grid side strips) counted chrome.

title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
code: hv_ggsave_dims(plot, width, height, units = c("in", "cm", "mm"))

title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
text: plot ggplot (patchwork) object. width Target panel content area width, units. height Target panel content area height, units. units One "", "cm", "mm". Default "".

title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
text: named list elements width, height, units — shaped splat directly ggplot2::ggsave() via .call() (see examples).

title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
text: Useful multi-panel figure sets constant data region required across PDFs regardless label length legend placement. Always measured PDF sizing device, units limited length (inches, cm, mm) — DPI irrelevant vector output.

title: Compute ggsave() dimensions for a fixed panel content area — hv_ggsave_dims
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(hp, mpg)) +   ggplot2::geom_point() +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Long title that eats vertical space",                 x = "Horsepower", y = "Miles per gallon")  dims <- hv_ggsave_dims(p, width = 4, height = 3) do.call(   ggplot2::ggsave,   c(list(filename = "fig.pdf", plot = p), dims) ) } # }

what: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
text: Validates stores pre-computed parametric curve data — optional Kaplan-Meier empirical overlay population life-table reference — hv_hazard object.  Pass result plot.hv_hazard() render figure.

title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
code: hv_hazard(   curve_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "survival",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL,   empirical = NULL,   emp_x_col = x_col,   emp_estimate_col = "estimate",   emp_lower_col = NULL,   emp_upper_col = NULL,   emp_group_col = group_col,   emp_geom = c("point", "step"),   reference = NULL,   ref_x_col = x_col,   ref_estimate_col = estimate_col,   ref_group_col = NULL )

title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
text: curve_data Data frame parametric predictions (fine grid). Typical source: SAS predict dataset exported CSV, sample_hazard_data(). x_col Name time / age column. Default "time". estimate_col Name predicted-value column: "survival", "hazard", "cumhaz". Default "survival". lower_col Lower CI column curve_data, NULL ribbon. Default NULL. upper_col Upper CI column curve_data, NULL. Default NULL. group_col Stratification column curve_data, NULL single curve. Default NULL. empirical Optional data frame KM empirical points (SAS plout dataset). Stored $tables$empirical. Default NULL. emp_x_col x column empirical. Defaults x_col. emp_estimate_col y column empirical. Default "estimate". emp_lower_col Lower error-bar column empirical, NULL. Default NULL. emp_upper_col Upper error-bar column empirical, NULL. Default NULL. emp_group_col Group column empirical. Defaults group_col. emp_geom Geom used empirical overlay: "point" (default, open circles) "step" (Kaplan-Meier step function). reference Optional data frame population life-table curves (SAS smatched dataset). Stored $tables$reference. Default NULL. ref_x_col x column reference. Defaults x_col. ref_estimate_col y column reference. Defaults estimate_col. ref_group_col Linetype-grouping column reference, NULL. Default NULL.

title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
text: S3 object class c("hv_hazard", "hv_data"); call plot() result render — see plot.hv_hazard(). object contains: $data (curve data frame), $meta (column-name mappings), $tables$empirical, $tables$reference.

title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
text: constructor covers complete tp.hp.dead.* SAS template family. column-name mappings three data frames fixed construction time; aesthetic parameters (ci_alpha, line_width, etc.) passed plot().

title: Prepare parametric hazard / survival data for plotting — hv_hazard
code: library(ggplot2)  dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 500, time_max = 10) emp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 500, time_max = 10, n_bins = 6)  # 1. Build data object hp <- hv_hazard(dat,   lower_col     = "surv_lower", upper_col = "surv_upper",   empirical     = emp,   emp_lower_col = "lower",     emp_upper_col = "upper" ) hp  # prints CI and empirical flags #> <hv_hazard> #>   x col       : time #>   estimate    : survival #>   CI          : surv_lower -- surv_upper #>   $data       : 500 rows × 10 cols #>   $empirical  : 6 rows  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_hazard p <- plot(hp)  # 3. Decorate: colour/fill palettes, axis scales, labels, theme p +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # Stratified groups -- colour scale adds clinical meaning dat2 <- sample_hazard_data(   n = 400, groups = c("No Takedown" = 1.0, "Takedown" = 0.65) ) hp2 <- hv_hazard(dat2,   lower_col = "surv_lower", upper_col = "surv_upper",   group_col = "group" ) plot(hp2) +   scale_colour_manual(     values = c("No Takedown" = "steelblue", "Takedown" = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- Global theme + RColorBrewer (set once per session) ------------------ if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ old <- ggplot2::theme_set(hv_theme_manuscript()) plot(hp2) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette   = "Set1", guide = "none") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival (%)") ggplot2::theme_set(old) } # }  # See vignette("plot-decorators", package = "hvtiPlotR") for theming, # colour scales, annotation labels, and saving plots.

title: Prepare longitudinal participation counts data for plotting — hv_longitudinal
code: hv_longitudinal(   data,   x_col = "time_label",   count_col = "count",   group_col = "series" )

title: Prepare longitudinal participation counts data for plotting — hv_longitudinal
text: object class c("hv_longitudinal", "hv_data"): $data validated input data frame. $meta Named list: x_col, count_col, group_col, n_timepoints, n_groups, n_obs. $tables Empty list.

title: Prepare longitudinal participation counts data for plotting — hv_longitudinal
code: dta <- sample_longitudinal_counts_data(n_patients = 300, seed = 42L)  # 1. Build data object lc <- hv_longitudinal(dta) lc  # prints group/time-point counts #> <hv_longitudinal> #>   Time points : 7 #>   Groups      : 2  (14 rows) #>   x / count / group : time_label / count / series  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_longitudinal library(ggplot2) p <- plot(lc, type = "plot")  # 3. Decorate: fill palette, y-axis scale, labels, theme p +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(Patients = "steelblue", Measurements = "firebrick"),     name   = NULL   ) +   scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma,                      breaks = seq(0, 2000, 500),                      expand = c(0, 0)) +   coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 2200)) +   labs(x = "Follow-up Window", y = "Count (n)") +   hv_theme("poster") +   theme(legend.position = c(0.85, 0.85))

what: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
title: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
text: Validates assembles propensity-score distributions mirrored histogram comparing treated group (bars axis) control group (bars axis), optional matched unmatched shading. Returns hv_mirror_hist object; call plot.hv_mirror_hist result obtain bare ggplot2 object.

title: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
code: hv_mirror_hist(   data,   score_col = "prob_t",   group_col = "tavr",   match_col = "match",   group_levels = c(0, 1),   group_labels = c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR"),   matched_value = 1,   score_multiplier = HVTI_SCORE_DEFAULT_MULTIPLIER,   binwidth = 5,   weight_col = NULL )

title: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
text: data data frame one row per patient. score_col Name propensity-score column (0–1 scale multiplier applied). Default "prob_t". group_col Name binary group-indicator column. Default "tavr". match_col Name binary match-indicator column (1 = matched). Default "match". group_levels Length-2 vector two values group_col (control first, treated second). Default c(0, 1). group_labels Length-2 character vector display labels corresponding group_levels. Default c("SAVR", "TF-TAVR"). matched_value Value match_col flags matched patient.  Default 1. score_multiplier Scalar applied score_col convert 0–100 display scale.  Default 100. binwidth Histogram bin width 0–100 scale. Default 5. weight_col Optional name IPTW weight column.  supplied, bar heights reflect weighted counts instead raw counts.

title: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
text: object class c("hv_mirror_hist", "hv_data"); call plot() result render figure — see plot.hv_mirror_hist.  list contains: $data Tidy data frame histogram bar coordinates plot.hv_mirror_hist. $meta Named list: score_col, group_col, match_col, group_labels, binwidth, lower, upper, y_breaks, n_obs, n_dropped. $tables Named list two elements: diagnostics named list diagnostic summaries. Always contains n_input, n_analyzed, n_dropped_missing_or_other_group, group_counts_before (table), score_summary_before (object), smd_before (numeric SMD). binary-match mode, additionally contains group_counts_matched, matched_rate_by_group, score_summary_matched, smd_matched. weighted IPTW mode, additionally contains effective_n_by_group smd_weighted. working per-patient data frame score rescaling complete-case filtering, used custom downstream diagnostics.

title: Prepare mirror-histogram data for plotting — hv_mirror_hist
code: dta <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(n = 500, separation = 1.5)  # 1. Build data object mh <- hv_mirror_hist(dta) #> mirror_histogram diagnostics: n=1000  dropped=0  [see $tables$diagnostics] mh                    # print diagnostics summary #> <hv_mirror_hist> #>   Groups      : SAVR (control) vs TF-TAVR (treated) #>   Score col   : prob_t #>   Match col   : match #>   N obs       : 1000  (dropped: 0) #>   Bin width   : 5 #>   Y range     : [-63, 66] #>   $tables     : diagnostics, working mh$tables$diagnostics # full diagnostics list #> $n_input #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $n_analyzed #> [1] 1000 #>  #> $n_dropped_missing_or_other_group #> [1] 0 #>  #> $group_counts_before #>  #>   0   1  #> 500 500  #>  #> $score_summary_before #> working$group: 0 #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   2.313  19.614  31.260  34.205  47.144  90.167  #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #> working$group: 1 #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   6.775  51.385  67.926  64.596  81.283  98.587  #>  #> $smd_before #> [1] 1.565275 #>  #> $group_counts_matched #>  #>   0   1  #> 230 230  #>  #> $matched_rate_by_group #>    0    1  #> 0.46 0.46  #>  #> $score_summary_matched #> working$group[matched_idx]: 0 #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   6.904  38.323  48.337  48.565  61.152  90.167  #> ------------------------------------------------------------  #> working$group[matched_idx]: 1 #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   6.775  38.321  48.425  48.872  61.136  89.236  #>  #> $smd_matched #> [1] 0.01837958 #>   # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_mirror_hist p <- plot(mh)  # 3. Decorate: axis labels and theme p +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Propensity Score (%)", y = "Count") +   hv_theme("poster")

what: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
title: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
text: Stores pre-computed NNT (ARR) data hv_nnt object.  Rows estimate_col NA (undefined ARR near zero) optionally removed construction via na_rm.  Pass result plot.hv_nnt() render plot.  Covers NNT component tp.hp.numtreat.survdiff.matched.sas.

title: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
code: hv_nnt(   nnt_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "nnt",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL,   na_rm = TRUE )

title: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
text: nnt_data Data frame pre-computed NNT estimates. See sample_nnt_data(). x_col Name time column. Default "time". estimate_col Name NNT (ARR) column. Default "nnt". lower_col Lower CI column, NULL. Default NULL. upper_col Upper CI column, NULL. Default NULL. group_col Grouping column multiple comparisons, NULL. Default NULL. na_rm Remove rows estimate_col NA storing $data. Applied construction time. Default TRUE.

title: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
text: S3 object class c("hv_nnt", "hv_data").

title: Prepare number-needed-to-treat data for plotting — hv_nnt
code: library(ggplot2)  nnt_dat <- sample_nnt_data(   n = 500, time_max = 20,   groups = c("SVG" = 1.0, "ITA" = 0.75) )  # 1. Build data object nn <- hv_nnt(nnt_dat, lower_col = "nnt_lower", upper_col = "nnt_upper") nn  # prints estimate column and CI flags #> <hv_nnt> #>   x col       : time #>   estimate    : nnt #>   CI          : nnt_lower -- nnt_upper #>   na_rm       : TRUE #>   $data       : 496 rows × 7 cols  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_nnt p <- plot(nn)  # 3. Decorate: axis scales, labels, theme p +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50), breaks = seq(0, 50, 10)) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Number Needed to Treat (NNT)") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 248 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`). #> Warning: Removed 27 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`).   # ARR curve -- same 3-step pattern with a different estimate column ar <- hv_nnt(nnt_dat, estimate_col = "arr",                lower_col = "arr_lower", upper_col = "arr_upper",                na_rm = FALSE) plot(ar) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 50),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Absolute Risk Reduction (%)") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 178 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Prepare nonparametric temporal trend curve data for plotting — hv_nonparametric

title: Prepare nonparametric temporal trend curve data for plotting — hv_nonparametric
text: curve_data Data frame; one row per (time, group) combination. x_col Name x-axis column. Default "time". estimate_col Name predicted value column. Default "estimate". lower_col Name lower CI bound column, NULL. Default NULL. upper_col Name upper CI bound column, NULL. Default NULL. group_col Name stratification column, NULL. Default NULL. data_points Optional data frame binned data summary points. Must columns matching x_col "value", plus group_col stratified. Default NULL.

title: Prepare nonparametric temporal trend curve data for plotting — hv_nonparametric
text: Covers full range tp.np.* SAS templates: SAS column mapping: estimate_col ← prev, mnprev, _p_, est_fev, est_z0d lower_col ← cll_p68 cll_p95 upper_col ← clu_p68 clu_p95 group_col ← indicator added wide--long reshape

title: Prepare nonparametric temporal trend curve data for plotting — hv_nonparametric
code: dat     <- sample_nonparametric_curve_data(n = 500, time_max = 12) dat_pts <- sample_nonparametric_curve_points(n = 500, time_max = 12)  # 1. Build data object np <- hv_nonparametric(dat, lower_col = "lower", upper_col = "upper",                           data_points = dat_pts) np  # prints CI / data-point flags #> <hv_nonparametric> #>   N curve pts : 500 #>   x / estimate: time / estimate #>   CI ribbon   : lower / upper #>   Data points : yes  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_nonparametric p <- plot(np)  # 3. Decorate: colour/fill palettes, axis scales, labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 12), breaks = 0:12) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.40),                               breaks = seq(0, 0.40, 0.10),                               labels = scales::percent) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Months", y = "Prevalence of AF") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 187 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`). #> Warning: Removed 53 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 3 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).   # --- Global theme (set once per session) ---------------------------------- if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ old <- ggplot2::theme_set(hv_theme_manuscript()) plot(np) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values   = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Months", y = "Prevalence of AF") # For multi-group curves swap scale_colour_manual with: #   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) #   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", guide = "none") ggplot2::theme_set(old) } # }  # See vignette("plot-decorators", package = "hvtiPlotR") for theming, # colour scales, annotation labels, and saving plots.

what: Prepare nonparametric ordinal outcome curve data for plotting — hv_ordinal

title: Prepare nonparametric ordinal outcome curve data for plotting — hv_ordinal

Default "time". estimate_col Name predicted probability column. Default "estimate". grade_col Name grade/category column. Default "grade". data_points Optional long-format data frame binned data summary points. Must columns matching x_col, "value", grade_col. Default NULL.

title: Prepare nonparametric ordinal outcome curve data for plotting — hv_ordinal

Build data object ord <- hv_ordinal(dat, data_points = dat_pts) ord  # prints grade count and data-point flag #> <hv_ordinal> #>   N curve pts : 2000  (4 grades) #>   x / estimate / grade : time / estimate / grade #>   Data points : yes  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_ordinal p <- plot(ord)  # 3. Decorate: colour palette, axis scales, labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(None     = "steelblue",                Mild     = "firebrick",                Moderate = "forestgreen",                Severe   = "goldenrod3"),     name = "TR Grade"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:5) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.50),                               breaks = seq(0, 0.50, 0.10),                               labels = scales::percent) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years", y = "Percent in each TR grade") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Warning: Removed 500 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). #> Warning: Removed 10 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

what: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
title: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
text: Validates wide cluster-assignment data frame, resolves node level ordering, computes default node colours supplied, pre-computes long-format Sankey data.  Call plot.hv_sankey result obtain bare ggplot2 Sankey diagram using ggsankey geoms.

title: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
code: hv_sankey(   data,   cluster_cols = paste0("C", 2:9),   node_levels = NULL,   node_colours = NULL )

title: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
text: data Data frame; one row per patient. Must contain columns named cluster_cols. cluster_cols Character vector column names giving cluster assignments value K, ascending K order. Default paste0("C", 2:9). node_levels Character vector giving display order node labels (bottom top within column). NULL (default), existing factor levels first cluster column used. node_colours Named character vector mapping node labels fill colours. NULL (default), colours drawn inline Set1 hex palette order c(2, 6, 8, 4, 3, 5, 7, 1, 9).

title: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
text: object class c("hv_sankey", "hv_data"): $data long-format Sankey data frame (four columns: x, node, next_x, next_node). $meta Named list: cluster_cols, node_levels, node_colours, n_patients, n_k. $tables Empty list.

title: Prepare cluster stability Sankey data for plotting — hv_sankey
code: dta <- sample_cluster_sankey_data(n = 300, seed = 42)  if (requireNamespace("ggsankey", quietly = TRUE)) {   # 1. Build data object   sn <- hv_sankey(dta)   sn  # prints cluster cols and node count    # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_sankey   p <- plot(sn)    # 3. Decorate: axis labels and theme   p +     ggplot2::labs(x = NULL, title = "Cluster Stability: K = 2 to 9") +     hv_theme("poster") } #> Warning: The `size` argument of `element_rect()` is deprecated as of ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use the `linewidth` argument instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggsankey package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/davidsjoberg/ggsankey/issues>.

what: Prepare spaghetti / profile data for plotting — hv_spaghetti

code: hv_spaghetti(   data,   x_col = "time",   y_col = "value",   id_col = "id",   colour_col = NULL )

Default "value". id_col Name subject-identifier column (used group aesthetic line continuity). Default "id". colour_col Name column map line colour, NULL single uniform colour. Default NULL.

Build data object sp <- hv_spaghetti(dta, colour_col = "group") sp  # prints subject count, observation count, column mapping #> <hv_spaghetti> #>   N subjects  : 150  (594 observations) #>   x / y / id  : time / value / id #>   Colour col  : group  # 2. what: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked
title: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked

title: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked
code: hv_stacked(   data,   x_col = "year",   group_col = "category",   binwidth = 1,   position = c("stack", "fill") )

title: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked
text: data data frame. x_col Name numeric column bin along x-axis. Default "year". group_col Name column whose distinct values define stacked groups.  coerced factor inside aesthetic mapping. Default "category". binwidth Width histogram bin, units x_col. Default 1. position Bar position: "stack" (raw counts, default) "fill" (proportions sum 1 within bin).

title: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked
text: object class c("hv_stacked", "hv_data"): $data validated input data frame. $meta Named list: x_col, group_col, binwidth, position, n_obs, n_groups. $tables Empty list.

title: Prepare stacked histogram data for plotting — hv_stacked
code: dta <- sample_stacked_histogram_data()  # 1. Build data object sh <- hv_stacked(dta, x_col = "year", group_col = "category") sh  # prints obs / group count #> <hv_stacked> #>   N obs       : 419  (3 groups) #>   x / group   : year / category #>   binwidth    : 1 #>   position    : stack  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_stacked p <- plot(sh)  # 3. Decorate: fill/colour brewer palette, axis labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   ggplot2::scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Category") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Year", y = "Count") +   hv_theme("poster")

what: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
text: Fits Kaplan-Meier (product-limit) Nelson-Aalen (Fleming-Harrington) survival model patient-level data returns hv_survival object containing tidy model output accessory tables.  plot built stage; call plot.hv_survival result obtain bare ggplot2 object can decorate scales, labels, hv_theme.

title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
code: hv_survival(   data,   time_col = "iv_dead",   event_col = "dead",   group_col = NULL,   method = c("kaplan-meier", "nelson-aalen"),   conf_level = 0.95,   report_times = c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25) )

title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
text: data data frame one row per patient. time_col Name numeric column holding follow-time (years). Default "iv_dead". event_col Name 0/1 logical event-indicator column. Default "dead". group_col Optional name character factor column used stratify analysis.  NULL (default) produces unstratified estimate labelled "". method Estimator: "kaplan-meier" (default, logit CI — mirrors SAS %kaplan) "nelson-aalen" (Fleming-Harrington cumulative hazard log CI — mirrors SAS %nelsont, preferred \(S(t)\) approaches zero). conf_level Confidence level CI band.  Default 0.95. report_times Numeric vector time points survival estimates numbers--risk tabulated. Default c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25).

title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
text: object class c("hv_survival", "hv_data") (list); call plot() result render figure — see plot.hv_survival. list three elements: $data Tidy data frame one row per (time, strata) pair. Columns: time, surv, lower, upper, n.risk, n.event, n.censor, cumhaz, strata, hazard, density, mid_time, life, proplife, log_cumhaz, log_time. $meta Named list: time_col, event_col, group_col, method, conf_level, report_times, n_obs, n_events. $tables Named list two data frames: risk (strata, report_time, n.risk) report (strata, report_time, surv, lower, upper, n.risk, n.event).

title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
text: SAS templates: tp.ac.dead.sas (%kaplan, %nelsont).

title: Prepare survival data for plotting — hv_survival
code: dta <- sample_survival_data(n = 500, seed = 42)  # 1. Build data object km <- hv_survival(dta) km               # print method shows key metadata #> <hv_survival> #>   Method      : kaplan-meier #>   Time col    : iv_dead #>   Event col   : dead #>   N obs       : 500  (events: 293, 58.6%) #>   Conf level  : 95% #>   Report times: 1, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 #>   $data       : 295 rows × 16 cols #>   $tables     : risk, report km$tables$report # survival estimates at report_times #>   strata report_time  surv     lower     upper n.risk n.event #> 1    All           1 0.954 0.9317320 0.9692443    478       1 #> 2    All           5 0.822 0.7859710 0.8530976    412       1 #> 3    All          10 0.642 0.5989814 0.6828473    322       1 #> 4    All          15 0.518 0.4741762 0.5615486    260       1 #> 5    All          20 0.414 0.3715880 0.4577269    207       0 #> 6    All          25 0.414 0.3715880 0.4577269    207       0 km$tables$risk   # numbers at risk #>   strata report_time n.risk #> 1    All           1    478 #> 2    All           5    412 #> 3    All          10    322 #> 4    All          15    260 #> 5    All          20    207 #> 6    All          25    207  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_survival p <- plot(km)  # 3. Decorate: axis scales, labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),                               labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20, 5)) +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 20), ylim = c(0, 100)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)",                 title = "Freedom from Death") +   hv_theme("poster") #> Scale for y is already present. #> Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.   # Stratified: colour scale adds clinical meaning dta_s <- sample_survival_data(   n = 500, strata_levels = c("Type A", "Type B"),   hazard_ratios = c(1, 1.4), seed = 42 ) km_s <- hv_survival(dta_s, group_col = "valve_type") plot(km_s) +   ggplot2::scale_color_manual(     values = c("Type A" = "steelblue", "Type B" = "firebrick"),     name   = "Valve Type"   ) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # Other plot types plot(km, type = "cumhaz") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years", y = "Cumulative Hazard") +   hv_theme("ppt")   plot(km, type = "loglog") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "log(Years)", y = "log(-log S(t))",                 title = "PH Assumption Check") +   hv_theme("ppt")   # --- Global theme + RColorBrewer (set once per session) ------------------ if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ old <- ggplot2::theme_set(hv_theme_manuscript()) plot(km_s) +   ggplot2::scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Valve Type") +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years after Operation", y = "Survival (%)") ggplot2::theme_set(old) } # }  # See vignette("plot-decorators", package = "hvtiPlotR") for theming, # colour scales, annotation labels, and saving plots.

what: Prepare survival difference (life-gained) data for plotting — hv_survival_difference

title: Prepare survival difference (life-gained) data for plotting — hv_survival_difference
code: hv_survival_difference(   diff_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "difference",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL )

title: Prepare survival difference (life-gained) data for plotting — hv_survival_difference
text: diff_data Data frame pre-computed survival differences. See sample_survival_difference_data(). x_col Name time column. Default "time". estimate_col Name difference column. Default "difference". lower_col Lower CI column, NULL. Default NULL. upper_col Upper CI column, NULL. Default NULL. group_col Grouping column multiple comparisons, NULL. Default NULL.

title: Prepare survival difference (life-gained) data for plotting — hv_survival_difference
text: S3 object class c("hv_survival_difference", "hv_data").

title: Prepare survival difference (life-gained) data for plotting — hv_survival_difference
code: library(ggplot2)  diff_dat <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("Control" = 1.0, "Treatment" = 0.70) )  sd <- hv_survival_difference(diff_dat,   lower_col = "diff_lower", upper_col = "diff_upper" ) plot(sd) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-5, 30),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hv_theme
text: Provides single entry point obtaining supported hvtiPlotR theme.

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hv_theme
code: hv_theme(   style = c("ppt", "dark_ppt", "light_ppt", "manuscript", "poster"),   ... )

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hv_theme
text: style Character keyword identifying theme style. Supported values: "ppt" / "dark_ppt" — dark background, white text (hv_theme_dark_ppt()); default PPT theme "light_ppt" — light/transparent background, black text (hv_theme_light_ppt()) "manuscript" — clean white background journal figures (hv_theme_manuscript()) "poster" — medium font conference posters (hv_theme_poster()) ... Additional parameters forwarded underlying theme constructor.

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hv_theme
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: hvtiPlotR Theme Generic — hv_theme
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) + geom_point()  p + hv_theme("manuscript")   # journal figure  p + hv_theme("poster")       # conference poster  p + hv_theme("light_ppt")    # light-background slide   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Dark PPT — best viewed against a dark slide background p + hv_theme("dark_ppt") +   ggplot2::theme(plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "navy")) } # }

what: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
title: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
text: large-font theme black panel background white text, suited dark-mode PowerPoint slides. default hvtiPlotR PPT theme — hv_theme_ppt() theme_ppt() aliases function. light-background variant use hv_theme_light_ppt(). Removes grid lines panel borders.

title: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
code: hv_theme_dark_ppt(   base_size = 32,   base_family = "",   header_family = NULL,   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "white",   paper = "transparent",   accent = "#3366FF" )  theme_dark_ppt(   base_size = 32,   base_family = "",   header_family = NULL,   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "white",   paper = "transparent",   accent = "#3366FF" )  theme_ppt(   base_size = 32,   base_family = "",   header_family = NULL,   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "white",   paper = "transparent",   accent = "#3366FF" )  hv_theme_ppt(   base_size = 32,   base_family = "",   header_family = NULL,   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "white",   paper = "transparent",   accent = "#3366FF" )

title: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
text: base_size Base font size points. Default 32. base_family Base font family. Default "" (device default). header_family Font family headers, NULL inherit base_family. Default NULL. base_line_size Line size used axis lines borders. Default base_size / 22. base_rect_size Rectangle border size. Default base_size / 22. ink Foreground (text line) colour. Default "white". paper Background colour. Default "transparent". accent Accent colour used theme_grey() elements. Default "#3366FF".

title: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: Dark PowerPoint Theme (default PPT theme) — hv_theme_dark_ppt
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) + geom_point()  # Dark PPT theme — large font, white text, black panel p + hv_theme_dark_ppt()   # Via alias p + theme_ppt()   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Best viewed against a dark slide background p + hv_theme_dark_ppt() +   ggplot2::theme(plot.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill = "navy")) } # }

title: Light PowerPoint Theme — hv_theme_light_ppt

what: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
title: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
text: Validates patient-level data frame, computes per-x-value summary statistics (mean median), returns hv_trends object. Call plot.hv_trends result obtain bare ggplot2 trend plot (LOESS smooth + annual summary points) can decorate colour scales, axis limits, hv_theme.

title: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
code: hv_trends(   data,   x_col = "year",   y_col = "value",   group_col = "group",   summary_fn = c("mean", "median") )

title: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
text: data Patient-level data frame (one row per patient). x_col Name numeric/integer time column (e.g. surgery year). Default "year". y_col Name continuous outcome column. Default "value". group_col Name grouping column, NULL single group. Default "group". summary_fn Function used compute per-x-point estimate: "mean" "median". Default "mean".

title: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
text: object class c("hv_trends", "hv_data"); call plot() result render figure — see plot.hv_trends. list contains: $data original patient-level data frame. $meta Named list: x_col, y_col, group_col, summary_fn, n_obs, n_groups. $tables List one element: summary — data frame per-x (per-group) summary statistics used point overlay.

title: Prepare temporal trend data for plotting — hv_trends
code: dta <- sample_trends_data(n = 600, year_range = c(1985L, 2015L),   groups = c("I", "II", "III", "IV"))  # 1. Build data object tr <- hv_trends(dta, summary_fn = "median") tr  # prints observation and group counts #> <hv_trends> #>   N obs       : 600  (4 groups) #>   x / y       : year / value #>   Group col   : group #>   Summary fn  : median  # 2. Bare plot -- undecorated ggplot returned by plot.hv_trends p <- plot(tr)  # 3. Decorate: colour palette, axis scales, labels, theme p +   ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(     values = c(I = "steelblue", II = "firebrick",                III = "forestgreen", IV = "goldenrod3"),     name = "NYHA Class"   ) +   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1985, 2015),                               breaks = seq(1985, 2015, 5)) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 80),                               breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(xlim = c(1985, 2015), ylim = c(0, 80)) +   ggplot2::labs(x = "Years", y = "%") +   hv_theme("poster")
