what: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
title: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
text: Writes one ggplot per slide PowerPoint file using officer::ph_with() rvg::dml() every plot lands editable DrawingML vector graphic — shapes, lines, text remain selectable PowerPoint. Plots placed via officer::ph_location() pixel-exact positioning; titles go designated title placeholder via officer::ph_location_type().

title: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
code: save_ppt(   object,   template = "../graphs/RD.pptx",   powerpoint = "../graphs/pptExample.pptx",   slide_titles = "Plot",   layout = "Title and Content",   master = NULL,   width = 10.1,   height = 5.8,   left = 0,   top = 1.2 )

title: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
text: object single ggplot object named/unnamed list ggplot objects. element produces one slide. template Path existing .pptx file used slide template. Default "../graphs/RD.pptx". powerpoint Output path new .pptx file. Default "../graphs/pptExample.pptx". slide_titles character vector slide titles. Recycled number plots: supply one string slides, one per plot. Default "Plot". layout PowerPoint slide layout name template. Default "Title Content". master PowerPoint master name template, NULL use template's first available master. Default NULL. width Plot width inches within slide. Default 10.1. height Plot height inches within slide. Default 5.8. left Distance inches left edge slide. Default 0.0. top Distance inches top slide. Default 1.2 (standard title bar).

title: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
text: Invisibly returns path given powerpoint.

title: Save ggplot Objects to an Editable PowerPoint Presentation — save_ppt
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  # Single plot — dark PPT theme matches a black-background slide p1 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "Miles per gallon", title = "Fuel economy") +   hv_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = p1,   template     = "graphs/RD.pptx",   powerpoint   = "graphs/fuel_economy.pptx",   slide_titles = "Fuel Economy by Weight" )  # List of plots — one slide per plot, individual titles p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mpg)) +   geom_boxplot() +   labs(x = "Cylinders", y = "Miles per gallon") +   hv_theme("dark_ppt")  save_ppt(   object       = list(p1, p2),   template     = "graphs/RD.pptx",   powerpoint   = "graphs/deck.pptx",   slide_titles = c("Scatter: fuel economy", "Box: mpg by cylinder count") )  # Manuscript (white background) for AATS-style presentations pm <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +   geom_point() +   labs(x = "Weight", y = "Miles per gallon") +   hv_theme("manuscript")  save_ppt(   object       = pm,   template     = "graphs/RD-white.pptx",   powerpoint   = "graphs/manuscript.pptx",   slide_titles = "Fuel Economy" ) } # }

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
text: Superseded. survival_difference_plot() superseded S3 constructor hv_survival_difference() plus plot.hv_survival_difference(). Plots difference survival two groups time, optional confidence band. Covers tp.hp.dead.life-gained.sas survival-difference component tp.hp.numtreat.survdiff.matched.sas. SAS context: HAZDIFL macro tp.hp.dead.life-gained.sas bootstraps difference S_2(t) - S_1(t) (S_1(t) - S_2(t)) stores result diffout dataset. Export dataset pass appropriate column names.

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
code: survival_difference_plot(   diff_data,   x_col = "time",   estimate_col = "difference",   lower_col = NULL,   upper_col = NULL,   group_col = NULL,   ci_alpha = 0.2,   line_width = 1 )

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
text: diff_data Data frame pre-computed survival differences. See sample_survival_difference_data(). x_col Name time column. Default "time". estimate_col Name difference column. Default "difference". lower_col Name lower CI column, NULL. Default NULL. upper_col Name upper CI column, NULL. Default NULL. group_col Name grouping column multiple comparisons, NULL. Default NULL. ci_alpha Transparency CI ribbon. Default 0.20. line_width Line width. Default 1.0.

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object.

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
text: SAS templates: tp.hp.dead.life-gained.sas, tp.hp.numtreat.survdiff.matched.sas.

title: Survival Difference (Life-Gained) Plot — survival_difference_plot
code: library(ggplot2)  diff_dat <- sample_survival_difference_data(   n = 500, time_max = 10,   groups = c("Control" = 1.0, "Treatment" = 0.70) )  # --- (1) Single comparison: TF-TAVR vs TA-TAVR (tp.hp.dead.life-gained) -- survival_difference_plot(   diff_dat,   lower_col = "diff_lower",   upper_col = "diff_upper" ) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue"), guide = "none") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-5, 30),                      labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")   # --- (2) Multiple treatment comparisons ---------------------------------- # Simulate three comparisons and combine (each row = one comparison) d1 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("Medical Mgmt" = 1.0, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 1 ) d1$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs Medical Mgmt"  d2 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("TA-TAVR" = 0.90, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 2 ) d2$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs TA-TAVR"  d3 <- sample_survival_difference_data(   groups = c("AVR" = 0.80, "TF-TAVR" = 0.70), seed = 3 ) d3$comparison <- "TF-TAVR vs AVR"  dall <- rbind(d1, d2, d3)  survival_difference_plot(dall, group_col = "comparison") +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = NULL) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", guide = "none") +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = 0:10) +   labs(x = "Years", y = "Survival Difference (%)") +   hv_theme("poster")

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Generated full audit test suite — March 2026

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: package ships 12 test files containing roughly 500 individual test_that() blocks. Every exported function least one test. overall suite strong several areas important gaps coverage depth, snapshot testing, handful plot families received single smoke test early integration work.

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Six plot families live entirely test_plot_integration.R 1–2 tests . input-validation tests, column-name flexibility tests, layer-structure assertions.

what: 2. Sample-data generators with no validation tests
title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: generators exported documented dedicated tests beyond passed plot function.

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: entire suite asserts structure (class, layer types, attribute names) never values. Drift estimated survival probabilities, computed SMDs, rendered label text go completely undetected. Targets expect_snapshot() (testthat 3.x): Rendered label text covariate_balance y-axis survival_curve report table fixed seed mirror_histogram diagnostics list fixed seed eda_plot plot title derived y_label

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: package’s value proposition involves composing outputs: survival_curve result styled hv_theme() exported via save_ppt(). test exercises full pipeline.

what: Priority 1 — Fill gaps in existing thin-coverage files (high ROI, low effort)
title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Create tests/testthat/test_hazard_plot.R tests/testthat/test_nonparametric_plots.R using established patterns test_kaplan_meier.R. file cover: Sample-data generator validation (columns, ranges, seed reproducibility, error paths) Plot function return type (expect_s3_class(p, "ggplot")) Layer structure (GeomLine, GeomRibbon, GeomPoint appropriate) Confidence interval flag changes layer count Column-name flexibility (non-default column names) Input validation (missing columns, non-data-frame input, --range parameters) Example — test_hazard_plot.R:
code: # tests/testthat/test_hazard_plot.R library(testthat)  # ============================================================================ # sample_hazard_data # ============================================================================  test_that("sample_hazard_data returns a data frame", {   df <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   expect_true(is.data.frame(df)) })  test_that("sample_hazard_data has required columns", {   df <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   expect_true(all(c("time", "hazard", "lower", "upper") %in% names(df))) })  test_that("sample_hazard_data hazard is positive", {   df <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   expect_true(all(df$hazard > 0)) })  test_that("sample_hazard_data confidence bounds straddle hazard estimate", {   df <- sample_hazard_data(n = 200, seed = 42)   expect_true(all(df$lower <= df$hazard + 1e-9))   expect_true(all(df$upper >= df$hazard - 1e-9)) })  test_that("sample_hazard_data is reproducible with same seed", {   df1 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 7)   df2 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 7)   expect_identical(df1, df2) })  test_that("sample_hazard_data differs across seeds", {   df1 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   df2 <- sample_hazard_data(n = 100, seed = 2)   expect_false(identical(df1, df2)) })  # ============================================================================ # hazard_plot — structure # ============================================================================  test_that("hazard_plot has a GeomLine layer", {   dat   <- sample_hazard_data(n = 50, seed = 1)   geoms <- sapply(hazard_plot(dat)$layers, function(l) class(l$geom)[1])   expect_true("GeomLine" %in% geoms) })  test_that("hazard_plot has a GeomRibbon layer (confidence interval)", {   dat   <- sample_hazard_data(n = 50, seed = 1)   geoms <- sapply(hazard_plot(dat)$layers, function(l) class(l$geom)[1])   expect_true("GeomRibbon" %in% geoms) })  test_that("hazard_plot with empirical overlay adds a GeomPoint layer", {   dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 50, seed = 1)   emp <- sample_hazard_empirical(n = 50, seed = 1)   p_no_emp   <- hazard_plot(dat)   p_with_emp <- hazard_plot(dat, empirical = emp)   expect_gt(length(p_with_emp$layers), length(p_no_emp$layers)) })  # ============================================================================ # hazard_plot — input validation # ============================================================================  test_that("hazard_plot errors when data is not a data frame", {   expect_error(hazard_plot(list(time = 1:5, hazard = 1:5)), "data.frame") })  test_that("hazard_plot errors when time_col is absent from data", {   dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 50, seed = 1)   expect_error(hazard_plot(dat, time_col = "nonexistent"), "not found") })  test_that("hazard_plot errors when hazard_col is absent from data", {   dat <- sample_hazard_data(n = 50, seed = 1)   expect_error(hazard_plot(dat, hazard_col = "nonexistent"), "not found") })

what: Priority 2 — Snapshot tests for value-sensitive outputs
title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Add tests/testthat/_snaps/ directory snapshot clinically important numeric outputs. Run testthat::snapshot_accept() establish baseline.
code: # In test_kaplan_meier.R — add near the bottom:  test_that("survival_curve report_table matches snapshot (fixed seed)", {   result <- survival_curve(     sample_survival_data(n = 500, seed = 42),     report_times = c(1, 5, 10, 15, 20)   )   expect_snapshot(attr(result, "report_table")) })  # In test_mirror_histogram.R:  test_that("mirror_histogram diagnostics match snapshot (fixed seed)", {   df     <- sample_mirror_histogram_data(200, seed = 42)   result <- suppressMessages(mirror_histogram(df))   expect_snapshot(attr(result, "diagnostics")) })

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Add relevant existing test files:
code: # In test_kaplan_meier.R — all-censored data: test_that("survival_curve handles all-censored data gracefully", {   dta <- sample_survival_data(n = 100, seed = 1)   dta$dead <- FALSE          # override: no events   expect_s3_class(survival_curve(dta), "ggplot") })  # In test_footnote.R — make_footnote prefix: test_that("make_footnote prepends prefix to text", {   pdf(NULL)   plot(1:10)   expect_no_error(make_footnote("analysis.R", prefix = "Source: "))   dev.off() })  # In test_eda_plots.R — logical vector: test_that("eda_classify_var handles a logical vector", {   result <- eda_classify_var(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, NA))   expect_true(result %in% c("Cat_Num", "Cat_Char")) })  # In test_save_ppt.R — mismatched slide_titles: test_that("save_ppt errors when slide_titles length mismatches list length", {   skip_if_not_installed("officer")   skip_if_not_installed("rvg")   template <- make_temp_template()   on.exit(unlink(template))   plots <- list(create_test_plot(), create_test_plot())   expect_error(     save_ppt(plots, template = template,              powerpoint = tempfile(fileext = ".pptx"),              slide_titles = c("A", "B", "C")),   # 3 titles for 2 plots     "slide_titles"   ) })

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
code: # tests/testthat/test_pipeline.R library(testthat) library(ggplot2)  test_that("full pipeline: survival_curve -> theme -> save_ppt", {   skip_if_not_installed("officer")   skip_if_not_installed("rvg")    template <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx")   officer::read_pptx() |>     officer::add_slide(layout = "Title and Content") |>     print(target = template)   on.exit(unlink(template))    km <- survival_curve(     sample_survival_data(       n = 100,       strata_levels = c("SAVR", "TAVR"),       seed = 1     ),     group_col = "valve_type"   )    pptx_out <- tempfile(fileext = ".pptx")   on.exit(unlink(pptx_out), add = TRUE)    expect_no_error(     save_ppt(       object       = km + hv_theme("ppt"),       template     = template,       powerpoint   = pptx_out,       slide_titles = "KM: SAVR vs TAVR"     )   )   expect_true(file.exists(pptx_out)) })

code: # In test_data.R — add after existing tests:  test_that("parametric dataset works with hazard_plot()", {   data(parametric, package = "hvtiPlotR", envir = environment())   # identify the expected column names from the docs   expect_s3_class(     hazard_plot(parametric),     "ggplot"   ) })  test_that("nonparametric dataset works with nonparametric_curve_plot()", {   data(nonparametric, package = "hvtiPlotR", envir = environment())   expect_s3_class(     nonparametric_curve_plot(nonparametric),     "ggplot"   ) })

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: categories worth testing: theme_man / theme_manuscript / theme_ppt identical-function tests — already covered via expect_identical(theme_man, theme_manuscript); need duplicate ggplot2 internal rendering — whether geom actually draws correctly ggplot2’s responsibility, hvtiPlotR’s Font availability — testing “Outfit” renders correctly every contributor’s machine worthwhile; existing base_family = "nonexistent" -error tests sufficient dev.() behavior — makeFootnote already tests adequately

title: hvtiPlotR Testing Strategy
text: Coverage report (requires covr):
code: # All tests devtools::test()  # A single file testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test_kaplan_meier.R")  # Update snapshots after intentional changes testthat::snapshot_accept()  # Full R CMD check (runs tests + examples + vignettes) devtools::check() covr::package_coverage() |> covr::report()

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9011
text: hv_ggsave_dims(): compute ggsave() width/height preserve fixed panel content area regardless axis labels, legend, title, facet strips. Returns named list shaped splat ggsave() via .call(ggsave, c(list(filename = ..., plot = p), dims)). Units length-("", "cm", "mm") since sizing device PDF.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9010
text: plot-functions.qmd: UpSet plot chunks (upset_data, upset_basic, upset_fill, upset_era) now skip Windows (eval: !expr   .Platform$OS.type != "windows"). ComplexUpset’s patchwork rendering crashes Rscript subprocess Windows CI runner (os error 232 / “pipe closed”), examples shown macOS Linux render reliably.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9009
text: Added scale_y_continuous(labels = abs) y-axis displays absolute counts halves panel. Replaced hard-coded y-coordinates annotate() calls y = Inf/y = -Inf plus vjust, anchoring group label near top/bottom panel edge regardless dataset size. Replaced hard-coded label strings ("SAVR", "TF-TAVR", etc.) mh$meta$group_labels[1] / [2], annotations always track labels supplied constructor.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9008
text: Vignettes: hv_theme("manuscript") calls inside R code blocks replaced hv_theme("poster") demonstrate non-default theme options. Prose references (e.g. migration guide comparison table) preserved unchanged. Theme-specific sections plot-decorators.qmd (## Manuscript, ## Manuscript PDF) continue demonstrate hv_theme("manuscript"). build hv_*(), (2) render bare ggplot p <- plot(obj), decorate scale_*() + hv_theme("poster"). plot-functions.qmd: UpSet section split ## Bare plot (showing plot(hu)) ## Applying theme (showing plot(hu) & hv_theme("poster")), explanatory note patchwork & operator required.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9007
text: hvti_survival() → hv_survival() hvti_trends() → hv_trends() hvti_mirror_hist() → hv_mirror_hist() hvti_spaghetti() → hv_spaghetti() hvti_balance() → hv_balance() hvti_alluvial() → hv_alluvial() hvti_sankey() → hv_sankey() hvti_nonparametric() → hv_nonparametric() hvti_ordinal() → hv_ordinal() hvti_followup() → hv_followup() hvti_longitudinal() → hv_longitudinal() hvti_stacked() → hv_stacked() hvti_eda() → hv_eda() hvti_hazard() → hv_hazard() hvti_nnt() → hv_nnt() hvti_upset() → hv_upset() hvti_theme() → hv_theme() hvti_survival_difference() → hv_survival_difference() is_hvti_data() → is_hv_data() Class strings "hvti_*" → "hv_*" (affects inherits() checks) package name (hvtiPlotR) unchanged.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9006
text: hv_mirror_hist() $tables$diagnostics: corrected return documentation “data frame matched/unmatched counts per group” accurately describe actual type — named list diagnostic summaries whose contents vary mode (binary-match vs weighted IPTW). keys now enumerated @return block. $meta keys hv_mirror_hist() return docs updated include keys actually stored (score_col, group_col, match_col missing). Added @family Propensity Score & Matching hv_mirror_hist() plot.hv_mirror_hist(), creating automatic bi-directional “See also” cross-links consistent hv_* constructor/plot pairs. plot.hv_mirror_hist() @return now describes composability + (scales, limits, labels, hv_theme), matching pattern used updated plot methods. plot.hv_mirror_hist() @seealso expanded descriptive text linked function, matching richer pattern used elsewhere.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9005
text: Added test_trends_plot.R (37 tests): $meta slot keys values, $tables$summary structure row counts, factor level order preservation, print.hv_trends output invisible return, full parameter coverage plot.hv_trends (se, span, point_size, point_shape, alpha, smoother, grouped vs ungrouped mapping, composability hv_theme). Added test_spaghetti_plot.R (25 tests): $meta slot keys values, id_col/y_col absent error cases, print.hv_spaghetti output without colour_col branch invisible return, full parameter coverage plot.hv_spaghetti (add_smooth, smooth_se, line_colour, line_width, alpha boundaries, y_labels error cases, smooth_method, grouped vs ungrouped mapping, composability hv_theme). Added test_hv_data.R (27 tests): new_hv_data() structure contract, input validation errors, is_hv_data() TRUE/FALSE relevant types, print.hv_data base-class output invisible return, subclass dispatch (verifying print.hv_spaghetti overrides print.hv_data), plot.hv_data fallback error subclass name message.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9004
text: hv_mirror() renamed hv_mirror_hist() naming consistency underlying plot type. old name registered @aliases entry ?hv_mirror still resolves correct help page.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9004
text: hv_mirror_hist() now searchable via ?mirror_histogram, ?hv_mirror, ??propensity, ??IPTW, ??matching @aliases @concept tags documentation.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9004
text: hv_* constructors plot.hv_* methods now carry @family tags, creating automatic bi-directional “See also” cross-links constructor plot method help system pkgdown reference. @return every constructor now explicitly says “call plot() render” links corresponding plot.hv_* method. @seealso entries across constructors plot methods now include descriptive text explaining role linked function. @examples main plot methods include \dontrun{} block demonstrating ggplot2::theme_set(hv_theme_manuscript()) applying publication theme globally, scale_colour_brewer() / scale_fill_brewer() multi-group colour palettes, pointer vignette("plot-decorators", package = "hvtiPlotR").

what: Breaking changes — new S3 constructor API
title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: plot functions replaced two-step S3 workflow: old single-call functions (mirror_histogram(), survival_curve(), etc.) removed. clean break; deprecated wrappers.
code: # Step 1: construct & validate obj <- hv_*(data, ...)          # returns c("hv_<concept>", "hv_data")  # Step 2: render plot(obj, ...) +                  # bare ggplot — no scales, labels, or theme   scale_colour_manual(...) +   labs(...) +   hv_theme("manuscript")

previous_headings: Breaking changes — new S3 constructor API
title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: hv_hazard() | hazard_plot() |hv_survival_difference() | survival_difference_plot() |hv_nnt() | nnt_plot() | legacy hazard helpers (hazard_plot(), survival_difference_plot(), nnt_plot()) remain exported marked Superseded — use S3 constructors instead.

previous_headings: Breaking changes — new S3 constructor API
title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Two constructors replace pairs old functions via type = argument plot(): hv_longitudinal — plot(x, type = "plot") (bar chart, longitudinal_counts_plot()) plot(x, type = "table") (text panel, longitudinal_counts_table()). hv_followup — plot(x, type = "followup") (death panel, goodness_followup()) plot(x, type = "event") (non-fatal event panel, goodness_event_plot()).

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Added hv_data S3 base class (R/hvti-data.R). Every hv_* constructor returns list(data=, meta=, tables=) class c("hv_<concept>", "hv_data"). new_hv_data() — internal constructor; validates data (data.frame), meta (named list), tables (list), subclass (character). print.hv_data() — fallback print method; shows class, dimensions, slot names. plot.hv_data() — fallback plot method; stops helpful message concrete plot.hv_*() registered. is_hv_data() — exported predicate.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Rewrote help.R package-level documentation describe new two-step constructor + plot() workflow list hv_*() constructors. Updated _pkgdown.yml reference index: grouped constructor family, plot.* print.* S3 methods explicitly listed. Updated vignettes (plot-functions.qmd, sas-migration-guide.qmd, plot-decorators.qmd) use new API throughout. Updated sas-migration-guide.qmd key-concepts section template reference table. Fixed stale @seealso cross-references orphaned old-API docblocks every migrated R source file.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Added tests/testthat/test_hazard_plot.R — full validation suite sample_hazard_data, sample_hazard_empirical, sample_life_table, hv_hazard, hv_survival_difference, hv_nnt (column checks, CI bounds, layer structure, multi-group, non-default column names, input validation, print output, empirical/reference validation). Added tests/testthat/test_nonparametric_plots.R — full suite sample_nonparametric_curve_data, sample_nonparametric_curve_points, nonparametric_curve_plot, sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data, sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points, nonparametric_ordinal_plot. Includes probability-sum--1 invariant test ordinal grades. Added tests/testthat/test_survival_derived.R — full suite sample_survival_difference_data, sample_nnt_data, legacy survival_difference_plot / nnt_plot. Covers NA-NNT t≈0 edge case cross-function time-grid consistency. Added tests/testthat/test_cluster_sankey.R — full suite sample_cluster_sankey_data cluster_sankey_plot. Validates hierarchical merge tree (C9=→ C2=) Ck exactly k levels. Added tests/testthat/test_pipeline.R — end--end pipeline tests covering survival_curve → hv_theme → save_ppt, multi-slide list pipelines, built-dataset usability, eda_classify_var edge cases (logical vector, -NA, length-1), composed multi-layer plots. Added snapshot test test_kaplan_meier.R survival_curve report_table fixed seed; added -censored single-observation edge-case tests. Added snapshot test test_mirror_histogram.R diagnostics fixed seed. Added slide_titles length-mismatch test test_save_ppt.R. Added make_footnote prefix-parameter tests test_footnote.R.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Fixed save_ppt() argument names throughout vignettes: plot = → object =, filename = → powerpoint =. Also added correct template = slide_titles = arguments missing. Fixed critical roxygen bug sample_mirror_histogram_data(): doc block used ##' (silently ignored roxygen2) instead #', function generated .Rd file. Converted ##' → #', modernised \code{} → backtick syntax, added @examples. Added @examples five theme functions: hv_theme(), hv_theme_manuscript(), hv_theme_dark_ppt(), hv_theme_light_ppt(), hv_theme_poster(). Expanded thin (2-line) @examples blocks four sample-data helpers: sample_life_table(), sample_nonparametric_curve_points(), sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(), sample_longitudinal_counts_data(). Fixed km$survival_plot km$risk_table accessor patterns vignettes/plot-decorators.qmd: survival_curve() returns ggplot attributes, named list. Replaced km (returned object survival plot) attr(km, "risk_table"). Fixed patchwork operator-precedence bug vignettes/plot-decorators.qmd: p_ms | p_km_ms + plot_layout(...) → (p_ms | p_km_ms) + plot_layout(...). Added patchwork Suggests DESCRIPTION (required vignettes/plot-decorators.qmd). Rewrote package-level help page (help.R / ?hvtiPlotR) document 57 exported functions, organised category. Expanded “Saving figures” section vignettes/sas-migration-guide.qmd correct save_ppt() single- multi-slide examples. Added ggplot2::geom_line(..., linewidth = 1.5) (replacing deprecated size =) updated remotes::install_github() (replacing devtools::install_github()) vignettes/hvtiPlotR.qmd.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: upset_plot() — added binary-column type check. ComplexUpset silently produces broken plots intersect columns contain non-binary values; function now errors clear message listing offending columns handing ComplexUpset. sample_stacked_histogram_data() — added start_year validation (previously n_years n_categories checked start_year ; non-integer non-finite value produced silently nonsensical output). trends_plot() — moved match.arg(summary_fn) data-frame column checks, users see clear data / column error rather opaque 'arg' one ... message data summary_fn wrong. validators.R — added two scalar-parameter helpers: .check_scalar_positive() (finite, positive) .check_scalar_nonneg() (finite, non-negative). cb_validate_params() covariate-balance.R now delegates four parameter checks helpers, eliminating 28 lines bespoke validation code.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: .NP_SIM constant list (nonparametric-curve-plot.R) — lifted seven simulation tuning constants (eta_intercept, logit_shift, cont_baseline, cont_scale, cont_sigma, eff_frac_prob, eff_frac_cont) local variables sample_nonparametric_curve_data() hard-coded defaults .np_sample_bins() single file-level private list. functions now reference .NP_SIM$* — change , updates simulation paths.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Standardised alpha range [0, 1] across plot functions. Previously survival_curve(), covariate_balance(), mirror_histogram(), spaghetti_plot(), goodness_followup_death_plot() / goodness_followup_event_plot() used (0, 1] (rejecting alpha = 0), alluvial_plot() used [0, 1]. functions now accept [0, 1] — alpha = 0 (fully transparent) valid ggplot2 value error. Added .check_alpha() shared validator R/validators.R. Enforces alpha ∈ [0, 1] call. = FALSE called every plot function accepts alpha argument. call. = FALSE sweep — every stop() call package now includes call. = FALSE error messages never expose internal function names callers. Expanded shared validators (R/validators.R) 11 files (3). alluvial-plot.R, covariate-balance.R, eda-plots.R, goodness-followup.R, hazard-plot.R, kaplan-meier.R, longitudinal-counts-plot.R, mirror-histogram.R, nonparametric-curve-plot.R, nonparametric-ordinal-plot.R, spaghetti-plot.R, stacked-histogram.R, trends-plot.R, upset-plot.R now delegate data.frame, column-presence, numeric-column, alpha checks .check_df(), .check_cols(), .check_col(), .check_numeric_col(), .check_alpha(). Error wording now consistent across entry points.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0
text: Named simulation tuning constants sample_nonparametric_curve_data() internal helper .np_sample_bins(): eta_intercept, logit_shift, cont_baseline, cont_scale, cont_sigma, eff_frac_prob, eff_frac_cont. Magic numbers replaced throughout single-curve, multi-group, binned-data-summary code paths. Named simulation tuning constants sample_nonparametric_ordinal_data() sample_nonparametric_ordinal_points(): a_first, a_step, eta_intercept. Every occurrence -0.2, 0.5, 1.2 replaced named constant. test_kaplan_meier.R: added five survival_curve error tests non-numeric time_col, invalid event_col values (character instead 0/1/logical), alpha 0, > 1, < 0. test_hazard_plot.R: added graceful-handling test empty data frame (correct columns, zero rows) — confirms ggplot renders without error. test_mirror_histogram.R: added error test non-numeric score_col.

title: hvtiPlotR 2.0.0.9000
text: Added eda_plot() — exploratory barplot/scatterplot single variable. Auto-detects variable type ("Cont", "Cat_Num", "Cat_Char") dispatches scatter + LOESS + rug (continuous) stacked/filled bar (categorical). NA values shown explicit "(Missing)" fill level. Returns bare ggplot object composition scale_fill_*, scale_colour_*, labs(), annotate(), [hv_theme()]. Ports Function_DataPlotting() tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots.R. Added eda_classify_var() — replicates UniqueLimit type-detection logic Barplot_Scatterplot_Function.R: classifies vector "Cont", "Cat_Num", "Cat_Char". Added eda_select_vars() — subsets reorders data frame character vector space-separated string column names. Replaces Order_Variables() Mod_Data <- dta[, Order_Var] pattern tp.dp.EDA_barplots_scatterplots_varnames.R. Added sample_eda_data() — mixed-type cardiac-surgery registry simulation (binary, ordinal, character-categorical, continuous variables) demonstrating eda_plot() eda_select_vars(). Reorganised inst/: moved par_cst.xpt npar_cst.xpt inst/extdata/ (standard R package location bundled data files); removed unreferenced presentation test artefacts (*.pptx, *.pdf, *.sas scratch files). Extended nonparametric_curve_plot() examples: added dual-Y-axis example (Example 10, \dontrun) using scale_y_continuous(sec.axis = ...); noted cll_p95/clu_p95 column availability 95 % CI (Example 2) per-group shape mapping via scale_shape_manual() (Example 4). Extended nonparametric_ordinal_plot() examples: added pre-operative severity comparison example grouping combined Mild/Moderate/Severe cohorts nonparametric_curve_plot(). Split vignette three: hvtiPlotR.qmd (SAS migration guide), plot-functions.qmd (per-function reference worked examples), plot-decorators.qmd (composition grammar: scale_*, labs(), themes, saving manuscript PDF, poster PDF, editable PowerPoint via save_ppt()).

title: hvtiPlotR 1.1.0
text: Added survival_curve() — Kaplan-Meier Nelson-Aalen survival analysis returning five plot types (survival, cumulative hazard, hazard, log-log, life/RMST) plus risk report tables. Ports SAS %kaplan %nelsont macros tp.ac.dead.sas. Added sample_survival_data() — realistic exponential survival simulation administrative censoring optional treatment strata. Added goodness_followup() — goodness--follow-scatter plot showing actual vs. potential follow-per operation year, optional non-fatal event panel. Added sample_goodness_followup_data() — simulates operative cohort operation dates, follow-times, competing events, death. Added covariate_balance() — standardised mean difference dot-plot propensity-score matching weighting diagnostics. Added sample_covariate_balance_data() — patient-level logistic simulation greedy 1:1 caliper matching; SMDs computed matching. Added stacked_histogram() sample_stacked_histogram_data() — stacked filled histogram numeric variable group. Improved mirror_histogram() sample data (sample_mirror_histogram_data()) use realistic logistic propensity-score model greedy 1:1 caliper matching optional ATE IPTW weights; extreme-PS patients naturally go unmatched. Added hv_plot() dispatcher supporting "mirror_histogram", "stacked_histogram", "covariate_balance" plot types. Added hv_theme() dispatcher "manuscript", "ppt", "dark_ppt", "poster" themes. Enabled roxygen Markdown (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)) **bold**, backtick code spans, [pkg::fn()] cross-references render correctly Rd help pages. Added survival package Imports. Updated package-level documentation (help.R) reflect current exported functions sample-data generators.

title: hvtiPlotR 0.2.2
text: Fixed deprecated ggplot2 syntax (size -> linewidth element_line element_rect). Removed empty save.hvtiplotr function. Fixed theme_dark_ppt pass parameters theme_grey. Updated documentation data objects. Updated README reference officer package instead deprecated ReporteRs.

title: hvtiPlotR 0.2.0
text: Initial CRAN submission.
